reading1.description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading2.description: Read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading3.description: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

reading4.description: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

example.firstSentence: I'm sure it was difficult for you to finish the project on time.
example.afterGap: difficulty finishing the project on time.

reading5.description: You are going to read an article. For questions 31–36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

reading6.description: You are going to read four short texts. For questions 37–40, choose from the texts (A–D). The text may be chosen more than once.

reading7.description: Six paragraphs have been removed from the text. Choose the correct paragraph (A–G) to fill each gap. There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.

reading8.description: You are going to read an article. For questions 47–56, choose from the sections (A–D). The sections may be chosen more than once.

listening1.description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

listening2.description: You will hear someone giving a talk. For questions 7–14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

listening3.description: You will hear a recording. For questions 15–20, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

listening4.description: You will hear five short extracts. You must complete two tasks while listening. For both tasks, choose from the list (A–H).

writing1.description: Write your answer in 220–260 words in an appropriate style.

writing2.description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220–260 words in an appropriate style.

speaking1.description: The examiner asks you questions about yourself, your opinions and your experiences. Answer naturally and try to develop your responses.

speaking2.description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

speaking3.description: Discuss the options shown with your partner for about two minutes, then decide together which two would be most effective.

speaking4.description: The examiner will ask you questions related to the theme from Part 3. Express and develop your opinions with your partner.
